title: Add support for Zulu timestamp parsing
body:
- Improve error message when podman varlink service is not running

Signed-off-by: Jhon Honce <jhonce@redhat.com>

comments.body: This should not conflict with Commit() support https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/798
Tests aren't hip.  Other than @wking's question, LGTM
LGTM
@jwhonce Gitvalidation is complaining about whitespace errors
@mheon Yeah, my editor is letting me down. :(
@rh-atomic-bot retry
The Travis failure has been happening consistently this afternoon. I don't think it's related to this PR.

With `datetime_parse` now such a thin wrapper around the external function, and with `datetime_parse` only used in our tests, do we want to drop `datetime_parse` from our public API and list `dateutil` only under [`tests_require`][1]?  That would make installation slightly lighter for folks who weren't planning on running the tests.

[1]: https://setuptools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/setuptools.html#new-and-changed-setup-keywords

@wking datetime_parse() is intended to be used by anyone trying to compare dates coming from the different backends vs. datetime.  You need it or at least it's code to write a script like: "perform operation on all containers created 30 days ago". If JSON had an idea of "type" I could hide the fact that that dates are all formatted differently. Instead I made these helpers to normalize dates and developers can use them as they wish.

> @wking datetime_parse() is intended to be used by anyone trying to compare dates coming from the different backends vs. datetime.

But can't folks that need that import `dateutil` (or whatever date-parsing implementation they like) and call it directly?

@wking If the parse() call was the default settings I would agree.  Since we need non-default settings, I believe there will be less support headaches if we encapsulate the call we want with the settings we need.  Knowing we're trading support costs for the dependency cost.  Our other python tools already use this library so packaging costs are zero.

> If the parse() call was the default settings I would agree.

Can we file issues for endpoints that return date-time strings which need `upper()` or `fuzzy`?  Improving those would help all Podman-over-Varlink consumers, and eventually get us both into "don't bother wrapping `dateutil`" agreement.

The rest of your analysis above makes sense to me.
mind adding the dep to the contrib/spec/... ?

@baude Updated.  Also added other runtime requires

+Requires: python3-setuptools
+Requires: python3-varlink
+Requires: python3-dateutil
LGTM
LGTM
@rh-atomic-bot r+
